evidence: "It was fun to watch Colin Firth as a deadly action hero."
"The script is silly. The directing is chaotic."
"The various heroic activities that took place outside the plane... were simply too ludicrous."
"Perhaps, good for a rainy day if you want to have chips and beer while using no part of your brain."
"I gave up with thirty minutes to go."
"The special effects could never have been called 'special'; they were simply awful."
"A good cast that was wasted on a bad script, bad directing, or all of the above."
"I don't know if it's a Hallmark movie but it has the feel of a Hallmark movie."
"I have watched several times to see if I could understand it."
"The actors/actresses did a good job with what they had to work."
"I can understand why some would find the musical score distracting."
"There's nothing offensive are of the sort to get us stirred up emotionally."
"Watched it all; wish I hadn't. No redeeming characters."

summary: The user shows a strong preference for simple narratives with a focus on drama and action, but they are highly critical of production quality. They tend to enjoy light-hearted content but express dissatisfaction with emotional engagement and pacing, often finding films lacking in substance.
